podcasts.name: Davie Fogarty
podcasts.title: How she made her first million
podcasts.transcript: What's your name and what do you do? I'm Sheena and I'm the founder of T S Teas. We're a functional wellness brand. How did you make your first million? >> I had to get my product in front of as many people as possible as cost-efficiently as possible. So, I did a bunch of podcasts. I would go to my local TV show. I did a lot of hard-earned PR, and that's how I made it my first million. Why do you think that worked versus everyone else that sells tea? So, I focused on that intersection of flavor and function and elevated experience. We actually created the world's first fully biodegradable and refillable tea collection, and uh it kind of meets that intersection of premium gifting wellness lifestyle and grocery. What was the most you've made in 1 year? >> Over 2 million. What's your number one tip for someone that wants to start a similar business? Go for something that's very experiential. Packaging matters. The market is so saturated, especially in the tea world, so you really have to stand out. >> you come up with the product idea? >> So, I was addicted to coffee and wine, honestly. So, I worked in hospitality. I was drinking seven or eight cups of coffee just to get through the crazy shifts, and then at night I would have wine to fall asleep and rinse and repeat. It wreaked complete havoc on my well-being. It amplified my anxiety, digestion, insomnia. So, when I discovered the world of tea, I realized however you're feeling, there's a tea for that. For sleep support, energy, digestion, gut support. So, I started blending my own teas to really support my own well-being. Tell me the story about how you actually got the first product to sell to the first customer. Honestly, I just made the product. It wasn't even pretty in the beginning. It wasn't really that interesting, but you know what? Like my packaging and my product itself has probably had more updates than iOS over the years, but I believe in progress over perfection, and I just got it out there, got feedback, and just kept iterating.
